info.title: ListBee
info.summary: One API call to sell and deliver digital content.
info.description:
ListBee is a commerce API for AI agents, n8n workflows, and automation platforms. Create a listing with a title, price, and content — get back a checkout URL. Buyers visit the URL, pay via Stripe, and receive the content automatically.

## Quick Start
1. `POST /v1/auth/otp` with your email — receive verification code
2. `POST /v1/auth/otp/verify` with the code — receive short-lived access token (`at_...`, 24h)
3. `POST /v1/api-keys` with `Authorization: Bearer at_...` — receive permanent API key (`lb_...`)
4. `POST /v1/listings` with title + price + content — use `Authorization: Bearer lb_...`
5. Share the checkout URL

## Authentication
Two token types:
- **Access token** (`at_` prefix): short-lived (24h), returned by `verify_otp`. Use to bootstrap (create API keys).
- **API key** (`lb_` prefix): permanent, returned by `create_api_key`. Use for all ongoing API calls.

Both are passed as: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

post.summary: Send a one-time password to an email address
post.description:
Step 1 of 3 in the authentication flow: send_otp → verify_otp → create_api_key.

Sends a 6-digit verification code to the given email. Works for both new and existing accounts — if no account exists, one will be created when the code is verified. The code expires in 5 minutes.

Next: call `verify_otp` with the code to receive a short-lived access token.

post.summary: Verify a one-time password and get an access token
post.description:
Step 2 of 3 in the authentication flow: send_otp → verify_otp → create_api_key.

Verifies the 6-digit code and returns a short-lived access token (`at_` prefix, 24 hours). If this is the first verification for this email, a new account is created (`is_new: true`).

The access token is NOT a permanent API key. Next: call `POST /v1/api-keys` with `Authorization: Bearer at_...` to create a permanent API key (`lb_` prefix). Use the permanent key for all subsequent API calls.

get.description: Account status and readiness. Check readiness.operational — if false, readiness.actions lists what's needed. readiness.next is the highest-priority action.

put.description: Update account profile and settings. Supports display_name, bio, avatar (file token from POST /v1/files), and ga_measurement_id.

delete.description: Permanently delete the authenticated account. PII is anonymized, API keys revoked, webhooks disabled. Order history is retained for accounting. This action is irreversible.

get.description: List all API keys for the authenticated account with full key values.

post.description:
Step 3 of 3 in the authentication flow: send_otp → verify_otp → create_api_key.

Create a permanent API key (`lb_` prefix). After verifying OTP, use the short-lived access token (`Authorization: Bearer at_...`) to call this endpoint. The returned `lb_` key is permanent — use it for all subsequent API calls.

Also works with an existing `lb_` key to create additional keys for other agents or integrations.

delete.description: Revoke an API key. Cannot delete the key you are currently authenticating with.

409.description: Cannot delete the key used for this request.

get.description: List customers (buyers) for the authenticated account. Customers are auto-created when a buyer completes their first order. Use this endpoint to get buyer email addresses for marketing, analyze purchasing patterns, or identify repeat customers. Sorted by most recent purchase first.

schema.description: Filter by exact buyer email address.

parameters.description: Filter by exact buyer email address.

parameters.name: created_after

schema.description: Only customers created after this ISO 8601 datetime.
schema.title: Created After

schema.description: Only customers created before this ISO 8601 datetime.

get.description: Get a single customer by ID. Customers are auto-created from order data — they represent unique buyer email addresses that have purchased from the seller.

post.description: Upload a file for use as a listing deliverable. Returns a file token valid for 24 hours. Pass the token to PUT /v1/listings/{listing_id}/deliverables or POST /v1/orders/{id}/fulfill to attach the file. Max size: 25 MB. Accepted types: PDF, EPUB, ZIP, images, audio, video, Office documents.

201.description: File uploaded. Token valid for 24 hours.

422.description: Unsupported file type or missing file.

post.description: Create a listing to sell digital content. Returns product page URL and readiness. If readiness.sellable is false, follow readiness.actions to resolve. readiness.next is the first action to take. When your account uses a Stripe test key, checkout URLs point to Stripe test mode.

422.description: Validation error (code: `validation_error`). Check `detail` for specifics: price must be positive, slug already exists, managed mode requires deliverables, deliverable URL unreachable or unsupported format, external mode cannot have deliverables.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Retry after the `Retry-After` header value.

get.description: List all listings for the authenticated account. Filter by status. Cursor-paginated. Each listing includes readiness — check readiness.sellable to see if it can accept orders.

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of listings to return.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of listings to return.

get.description: Get a single listing by ID. Check readiness.sellable — if false, readiness.actions tells you what's needed.

put.description: Update listing fields. Slug can be changed while in draft status — input is slugified, conflicts get a random suffix. Returns updated listing with readiness.

delete.description: Delete a listing by ID and its stored content. Irreversible.

put.description: Replace all deliverables on a draft listing. Accepts an array of up to 3 deliverables (file, url, text). File tokens must be obtained via POST /v1/files first. Old file deliverables are automatically cleaned up from storage.

404.description: Listing not found or not owned by this account.

409.description: Listing is not in draft status.

422.description: Validation error (too many, invalid token, etc).

post.description: Add a single deliverable to a draft listing. Appends to the existing array. Returns the new deliverable with a unique del_ prefixed ID. Max 3 deliverables per listing — returns 409 if at capacity.

409.description: Listing not in draft, or at max capacity.

delete.description: Remove all deliverables from a draft listing. Demotes the listing to external fulfillment. Draft only — returns 409 if the listing is published.

409.description: Listing is not in draft state.

delete.description: Remove a single deliverable by its del_ ID from a draft listing. If the deliverable is a file, the CDN content is cleaned up.

409.description: Listing is not in draft status.

post.description: Validate and publish a draft listing. Transitions to published state — the listing becomes live, buyable, and immutable. Fails with 409 and readiness actions if requirements are not met.

get.description: List orders for the authenticated account. Filter by status, listing, and date range. Paginated. Order lifecycle: PENDING (checkout started, buyer data captured) → PAID (payment confirmed, order.paid webhook fires) → FULFILLED (content delivered via ListBee or order closed via POST /fulfill). PAID is terminal for external orders where delivery is handled outside ListBee. Terminal error states: CANCELED (payment failed or abandoned), FAILED (system error). Managed listings auto-fulfill on payment. External listings stay in PAID until the seller calls POST /fulfill.

parameters.name: buyer_email

schema.description: Filter by exact buyer email address.
schema.title: Buyer Email

parameters.description: Filter by exact buyer email address.

parameters.name: created_after

schema.description: Only orders created after this ISO 8601 datetime.
schema.title: Created After

parameters.description: Only orders created after this ISO 8601 datetime.

parameters.name: created_before

schema.description: Only orders created before this ISO 8601 datetime.
schema.title: Created Before

parameters.description: Only orders created before this ISO 8601 datetime.

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of orders to return.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of orders to return.

get.description: Get a single order by ID. Order lifecycle: PENDING → PAID → FULFILLED. PAID is terminal for external orders where delivery is handled outside ListBee. FULFILLED means content was delivered via ListBee or the order was explicitly closed via POST /fulfill. Managed listings auto-fulfill on payment.

post.description: Issue a full refund for an order. The refund is processed through Stripe on the seller's connected account. Only orders with status `paid` or `fulfilled` can be refunded. If the order is already refunded, returns the order as-is (idempotent). The order's refund_amount and refunded_at update asynchronously via Stripe webhook.

422.description: Order cannot be refunded (wrong status or no payment intent).

post.description: Fulfill an order by delivering digital content or marking it complete. Include `deliverables` to deliver files, URLs, or text — ListBee creates an access grant, emails the buyer a download link, and transitions the order to FULFILLED. Omit `deliverables` to close out an order that was handled externally (bookkeeping only, no buyer email). Only valid for orders in PAID state on listings without pre-set deliverables (external fulfillment). Idempotent: returns 200 if already FULFILLED.

get.description: Authenticated connectivity check. Returns ok if the API key is valid and the API is reachable.

post.description: Start Stripe Connect guided onboarding. Returns a URL to redirect the human to. The human completes Stripe's identity and bank account verification flow. On completion, Stripe fires an account.updated webhook and ListBee marks the account as connected. The stripe_account_id is saved immediately — payment_config is set only after webhook confirmation.

delete.description: Remove Stripe configuration from the account. The account will no longer accept payments until a new Connect session is completed.

get.description: List all webhook endpoints for the authenticated account. Use POST /v1/webhooks to register new endpoints. All order event payloads include the full order snapshot — no follow-up API calls needed.

post.description: Register a webhook endpoint. Returns the endpoint with its signing secret — store it, shown once. All order events (order.paid, order.fulfilled, order.refunded, order.disputed, order.dispute_closed, order.canceled) include a full order snapshot in the payload: order_id, listing_id, listing_slug, buyer_email, amount, currency, status, checkout_data, shipping_address, timestamps, and deliverable info. Agents never need a follow-up API call after receiving a webhook. See the WebhookPayloadExample schema for the full payload shape.

put.description: Update a webhook endpoint. Only provided fields are changed.

get.description: List delivery event history for a webhook endpoint. Use to debug failed deliveries.

parameters.name: delivered

schema.description: Filter by delivery status. `true` for delivered, `false` for pending/failed.
schema.title: Delivered

parameters.description: Filter by delivery status. `true` for delivered, `false` for pending/failed.

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of events to return.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return.

post.description: Reset a failed webhook event and re-dispatch for immediate delivery. Clears attempts, failed_at, last_error, and delivered_at, then enqueues a new delivery.
Send a test event to a webhook endpoint and return the delivery result. Does not persist an event record.

operational.title: Operational
operational.description: True when the account can sell. False means actions are needed.

actions.title: Actions
actions.description: What's needed to make this account operational. Empty when operational is true.

display_name.title: Display Name
display_name.description: Seller name shown on product pages.

bio.title: Bio
bio.description: Short seller bio shown on product pages.
examples: We create premium digital tools for modern marketers.

has_avatar.title: Has Avatar
has_avatar.description: true if avatar image exists. Upload via POST /v1/files, set via PUT /v1/account.

plan.title: Plan
plan.description: Current plan tier. Values: free (15% fee), growth (5% fee), scale (3% fee).

fee_rate.title: Fee Rate
fee_rate.description: Transaction fee rate as decimal string. Determined by plan: free=0.15, growth=0.05, scale=0.03. Applied as platform fee on each order.

billing_status.title: Billing Status
billing_status.description: Subscription billing status. `active` = payments current. `past_due` = payment failed, service continues during grace period. `unpaid` = grace period expired, listings may be blocked.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: Account currency (ISO 4217, lowercase). Set automatically from Stripe Connect. Cannot be changed after connection.

ga_measurement_id.title: Ga Measurement Id
ga_measurement_id.description: Google Analytics 4 Measurement ID (G-XXXXXXXXXX). Set via PUT /v1/account. Injected on all your product pages and thank-you pages

notify_orders.title: Notify Orders
notify_orders.description: Whether seller receives email alerts for new external orders

readiness.description: Account operational readiness. `operational` is true when the account can sell. If false, `actions` lists what's needed with kind (api/human), resolve details, and `next` points to the highest-priority action.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Time at which the account was created

total_revenue.title: Total Revenue
total_revenue.description: Total revenue in smallest currency unit (completed orders)

total_orders.title: Total Orders
total_orders.description: Total number of orders this month

code.description: Machine-readable action identifier for automation branching.

kind.description: Whether an agent can execute this directly (api) or needs human interaction (human).

message.title: Message
message.description: Human-readable explanation. AI agents relay this to users.
examples: Set Stripe secret key to accept payments

method.title: Method
method.description: How to resolve: HTTP method (POST, GET) for kind=api, 'redirect' for kind=human.

endpoint.title: Endpoint
endpoint.description: API endpoint path for kind=api actions.

url.title: Url
url.description: Full URL for kind=human actions (browser redirect).

params.title: Params
params.description: Example request body for kind=api actions.

type.description: Deliverable type: `file` (uploaded via POST /v1/files), `url` (redirect), or `text` (literal string).

token.title: Token
token.description: File token from POST /v1/files. Required when type is `file`.

value.title: Value
value.description: URL (https required) or text content. Required when type is `url` or `text`.

AddDeliverableRequest.title: AddDeliverableRequest
AddDeliverableRequest.description: Add a single deliverable to a listing.

has_more.title: Has More
has_more.description: Always false — API keys are not paginated.

cursor.title: Cursor
cursor.description: Always null — API keys are not paginated.

prefix.title: Prefix
prefix.description: First 8 characters of the key value

key.title: Key
key.description: Full API key value. Use as Bearer token for all authenticated requests.

access_token.title: Access Token
access_token.description: Short-lived access token (`at_` prefix, expires in 24 hours). This is NOT a permanent API key. To get a permanent key: call `POST /v1/api-keys` with `Authorization: Bearer at_...` — the returned `lb_` key is permanent and should be used for all subsequent API calls.

expires_in.title: Expires In
expires_in.description: Seconds until the access token expires.

is_new.title: Is New
is_new.description: True if this verification created a new account. False for returning accounts.

account.description: The authenticated account with readiness status.

sort_order.title: Sort Order
sort_order.description: Display order. Lower values shown first. Fields with equal sort_order are ordered by position in the array.

CheckoutFieldInput.title: CheckoutFieldInput
CheckoutFieldInput.description: A field to collect from the buyer at checkout.

CheckoutFieldType.title: CheckoutFieldType
CheckoutFieldType.description: Types of fields that can be collected at checkout.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable label for this API key.

checkout_schema.title: Checkout Schema
checkout_schema.description: Custom fields to collect from the buyer at checkout (e.g. size, color, shipping address). Email is always collected.

name.title: Name
name.description: Product name shown on the product page

price.title: Price
price.description: Price in cents (smallest currency unit). Examples: $5 = 500, $29 = 2900, $99.99 = 9999.

stock.title: Stock
stock.description: Stock quantity. null = unlimited (default), 0 = out of stock, positive integer = sell N then stop. Auto-decrements on each order.

description.title: Description
description.description: Product description shown on the product page. Improves buyer conversion. Plain text, max 5000 chars.
examples: A comprehensive guide to modern SEO techniques.

tagline.title: Tagline
tagline.description: Short tagline shown below the product name on the product page. Max 140 chars.

highlights.title: Highlights
highlights.description: Bullet-point features shown as badges on the product page. Max 10 items. Each should be short (2-4 words).

cta.title: Cta
cta.description: Buy button text on the product page. Defaults to 'Buy Now' if omitted. Max 60 chars.

cover.title: Cover
cover.description: Cover image file token from POST /v1/files. Shown prominently on the product page. Recommended: 1200x630px.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs forwarded in webhook event payloads. Use for your own tracking (campaign IDs, source tags).

utm_source.title: Utm Source
utm_source.description: UTM source tag for Google Analytics. Defaults to 'listbee' if omitted

utm_medium.title: Utm Medium
utm_medium.description: UTM medium tag for Google Analytics. Defaults to 'product_page' if omitted

utm_campaign.title: Utm Campaign
utm_campaign.description: UTM campaign tag for Google Analytics. Defaults to the listing slug if omitted

compare_at_price.title: Compare At Price
compare_at_price.description: Strikethrough price in cents (smallest currency unit). Must be greater than price. Examples: $39 = 3900.

badges.title: Badges
badges.description: Short promotional badges shown on the product page (e.g. 'Best seller', 'Limited time'). Max 10 items.

cover_blur.description: Cover image blur mode. 'true' always blurs, 'false' never blurs, 'auto' blurs when deliverable is an image file.

rating.title: Rating
rating.description: Seller-provided aggregate star rating (1-5). Shown on the product page. Values outside range clamped.

rating_count.title: Rating Count
rating_count.description: Seller-provided review or purchase count shown alongside the rating on the product page.

reviews.title: Reviews
reviews.description: Featured review cards shown on the product page. Seller-provided, not buyer-submitted. Max 10.

examples.content: Best decision I've made for my health.
examples.name: Clara D.

faqs.title: Faqs
faqs.description: FAQ accordion shown on the product page. Answers common buyer questions. Max 10 items.

CreateListingRequest.title: CreateListingRequest
CreateListingRequest.description: All optional display fields (name, description, tagline, highlights, cta, badges, cover, reviews, faqs) appear on the product page buyers see.

name.title: Name
name.description: A name to help you identify this endpoint in the dashboard

url.title: Url
url.description: The HTTPS URL that will receive POST requests for each event

events.title: Events
events.description: Event types to subscribe to. Empty = all events. Available: order.paid, order.fulfilled, order.shipped, order.refunded, order.disputed, order.dispute_closed, order.canceled, listing.created, listing.updated, listing.out_of_stock, listing.deleted.

total_orders.title: Total Orders
total_orders.description: Total number of completed orders from this buyer.

total_spent.title: Total Spent
total_spent.description: Total amount spent in cents (smallest currency unit).

last_order_at.title: Last Order At
last_order_at.description: When this buyer most recently purchased.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: When this customer record was created.

type.description: Deliverable type: `file` (uploaded via POST /v1/files), `url` (redirect), or `text` (literal string).

token.title: Token
token.description: File token from POST /v1/files. Required when type is `file`.

value.title: Value
value.description: URL (https required) or text content. Required when type is `url` or `text`.

DeliverableInputRequest.title: DeliverableInputRequest
DeliverableInputRequest.description: Typed deliverable input. Shared by PUT /deliverables and POST /fulfill.

size.title: Size
size.description: File size in bytes (file type only).

id.title: Id
id.description: File token. Pass as 'token' to set_deliverables, or as 'cover'/'avatar' to create/update listing/account.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.description: File purpose: 'deliverable' (listing content), 'cover' (listing cover image), or 'avatar' (account avatar).

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: Token expires at this time. Upload a new file after expiry.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Time at which the file was uploaded

deliverables.title: Deliverables
deliverables.description: Deliverables to attach. Max 3, mixed types. Omit for close-out (no content delivery).

FulfillOrderRequest.title: FulfillOrderRequest
FulfillOrderRequest.description:
Fulfill an order. Include deliverables to deliver digital content via ListBee.
Omit deliverables to mark the order as complete (close-out).

total_count.title: Total Count
total_count.description: Total number of matching resources across all pages

cursor.description: Pass as `cursor` query param to fetch the next page

has_more.description: True if more results exist beyond this page

highlights.title: Highlights

cta.title: Cta
cta.description: Buy button text on the product page. Defaults to 'Buy Now' if omitted.

price.title: Price
price.description: Price in cents (smallest currency unit). Examples: $5 = 500, $29 = 2900, $99.99 = 9999.

deliverables.title: Deliverables
deliverables.description: Array of deliverables attached to this listing. Empty for external fulfillment.

has_deliverables.title: Has Deliverables
has_deliverables.description: `true` if all deliverables have content and listing uses managed fulfillment

compare_at_price.title: Compare At Price

badges.title: Badges

cover_blur.description: Cover image blur mode. 'true' always blurs, 'false' never blurs, 'auto' blurs when deliverable is an image file.

rating.title: Rating
rating.description: Seller-provided aggregate star rating (1-5). Shown on the product page.

rating_count.title: Rating Count
rating_count.description: Seller-provided review or purchase count shown alongside the rating on the product page.

reviews.title: Reviews
reviews.description: Featured review cards shown on the product page. Seller-provided, not buyer-submitted.

faqs.title: Faqs
faqs.description: FAQ accordion shown on the product page. Answers common buyer questions.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs forwarded in webhook event payloads. Set at creation or update.

utm_source.title: Utm Source
utm_source.description: UTM source tag for Google Analytics

utm_medium.title: Utm Medium
utm_medium.description: UTM medium tag for Google Analytics

utm_campaign.title: Utm Campaign
utm_campaign.description: UTM campaign tag for Google Analytics

stock.title: Stock
stock.description: Stock quantity. null = unlimited, 0 = out of stock. Auto-decrements on each order.

url.title: Url
url.description: Full product page URL — share this with buyers

embed_url.title: Embed Url
embed_url.description: Embeddable product page URL — use in iframes. Renders minimal version without header/footer.

readiness.description: Monetization readiness. `sellable` is true when buyers can complete a purchase. If false, `actions` lists what's needed with kind (api/human), resolve details, and `next` points to the highest-priority action.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Time at which the listing was created

total_count.title: Total Count
total_count.description: Total number of matching resources across all pages

cursor.title: Cursor
cursor.description: Pass as `cursor` query param to fetch the next page

has_more.title: Has More
has_more.description: True if more results exist beyond this page

buyer_email.title: Buyer Email
buyer_email.description: Buyer's email address collected at Stripe Checkout. Use for customer communication.

amount.title: Amount
amount.description: Amount paid in cents (smallest currency unit). $29.00 = 2900.

status.description: Current order status. Lifecycle: PENDING → PAID → FULFILLED. PAID is terminal for external orders (seller handles delivery outside ListBee). FULFILLED means content delivered via ListBee or order closed via POST /fulfill. Terminal error states: CANCELED, FAILED.

checkout_data.title: Checkout Data
checkout_data.description: Custom checkout field values submitted by the buyer. Keys match the listing's checkout_schema.

deliverables.title: Deliverables
deliverables.description: Deliverables attached to this order. Auto-populated for managed fulfillment on payment, or pushed via POST /fulfill for dynamic fulfillment.

stripe_payment_intent_id.title: Stripe Payment Intent Id
stripe_payment_intent_id.description: Stripe PaymentIntent ID. Use for cross-referencing with Stripe dashboard or API.

platform_fee.title: Platform Fee
platform_fee.description: Platform fee in cents deducted from this order. Amount seller receives = amount - platform_fee.

refund_amount.title: Refund Amount
refund_amount.description: Amount refunded to buyer in cents. 0 if not refunded.

refunded_at.title: Refunded At
refunded_at.description: When the refund was processed. null if not refunded.

dispute_status.title: Dispute Status
dispute_status.description: Stripe dispute status if disputed. Values: needs_response, under_review, won, lost.

dispute_reason.title: Dispute Reason
dispute_reason.description: Reason for the dispute as reported by the buyer's bank.

dispute_amount.title: Dispute Amount
dispute_amount.description: Disputed amount in cents. 0 if not disputed.

disputed_at.title: Disputed At
disputed_at.description: When the dispute was opened. null if not disputed.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Time at which the order was created

email.title: Email
email.description: Email the verification code was sent to.

expires_in.title: Expires In
expires_in.description: Seconds until the verification code expires.

message.title: Message
message.default: Check your email for a 6-digit verification code.
examples: Check your email for a 6-digit verification code.

type.title: Type
type.description: URI identifying the error type. Points to documentation.

title.title: Title
title.description: Short, stable, human-readable label for the error category.

status.title: Status
status.description: HTTP status code echoed in the body.

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: Specific explanation of what went wrong.

code.title: Code
code.description: Machine-readable error code for programmatic handling.

param.title: Param
param.description: The request field that caused the error, if applicable.

ProblemDetail.title: ProblemDetail
ProblemDetail.description: RFC 9457 Problem Details for HTTP APIs.

rating.title: Rating
rating.description: Star rating (1–5). Values outside this range are clamped automatically

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address to send the verification code to. Works for both new and existing accounts — if no account exists for this email, one will be created when the code is verified.

deliverables.title: Deliverables
deliverables.description: Array of deliverables. Max 3. Mixed types allowed.

SetDeliverablesRequest.title: SetDeliverablesRequest
SetDeliverablesRequest.description: Replace all deliverables on a listing.

url.title: Url
url.description: Stripe onboarding URL. Redirect the human here.

display_name.title: Display Name
display_name.description: Seller name shown on product pages. Improves buyer trust and conversion. If not set, buyers see a generic seller label.

bio.title: Bio
bio.description: Short seller bio shown on product pages. Plain text. Helps buyers understand who they're buying from.
examples: We create premium digital tools for modern marketers.

avatar.title: Avatar
avatar.description: Avatar image file token from POST /v1/files. Replaces any existing avatar. Shown on product pages next to seller name.

ga_measurement_id.title: Ga Measurement Id
ga_measurement_id.description: Google Analytics 4 Measurement ID (G-XXXXXXXXXX). Set to null to remove

notify_orders.title: Notify Orders
notify_orders.description: Email me when I receive a new external order

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: URL slug for the product page (e.g. 'seo-playbook'). Only changeable in draft. Auto-slugified. On conflict, random suffix appended.

price.title: Price
price.description: Price in cents (smallest currency unit). Examples: $5 = 500, $29 = 2900, $99.99 = 9999.

stock.title: Stock
stock.description: Stock quantity. null = unlimited, 0 = out of stock, positive integer = sell N then stop.

checkout_schema.title: Checkout Schema
checkout_schema.description: Custom fields to collect from the buyer at checkout. Replaces existing schema.

description.title: Description
description.description: Product description shown on the product page. Improves buyer conversion. Plain text, max 5000 chars.
examples: A comprehensive guide to modern SEO.

tagline.title: Tagline
tagline.description: Short tagline shown below the product name on the product page. Max 140 chars.

highlights.title: Highlights
highlights.description: Bullet-point features shown as badges on the product page. Max 10 items. Each should be short (2-4 words).

cta.title: Cta
cta.description: Buy button text on the product page. Defaults to 'Buy Now' if omitted. Max 60 chars.

cover.title: Cover
cover.description: Cover image file token from POST /v1/files. Shown prominently on the product page. Recommended: 1200x630px.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs forwarded in webhook event payloads. Use for your own tracking (campaign IDs, source tags).

utm_source.title: Utm Source
utm_source.description: UTM source tag for Google Analytics

utm_medium.title: Utm Medium
utm_medium.description: UTM medium tag for Google Analytics

utm_campaign.title: Utm Campaign
utm_campaign.description: UTM campaign tag for Google Analytics

compare_at_price.title: Compare At Price
compare_at_price.description: Strikethrough price in cents (smallest currency unit). Must be greater than price. Examples: $39 = 3900.

badges.title: Badges
badges.description: Short promotional badges shown on the product page (e.g. 'Best seller', 'Limited time'). Max 10 items.

cover_blur.description: Cover image blur mode. 'true' always blurs, 'false' never blurs, 'auto' blurs when deliverable is an image file.

rating.title: Rating
rating.description: Seller-provided aggregate star rating (1-5). Shown on the product page. Values outside range clamped.

rating_count.title: Rating Count
rating_count.description: Seller-provided review or purchase count shown alongside the rating on the product page.

reviews.title: Reviews
reviews.description: Featured review cards shown on the product page. Seller-provided, not buyer-submitted. Max 10.

faqs.title: Faqs
faqs.description: FAQ accordion shown on the product page. Answers common buyer questions. Max 10 items.

UpdateListingRequest.title: UpdateListingRequest
UpdateListingRequest.description: Partial update — only provided fields are changed.

events.title: Events
events.description: Replaces the current event subscriptions. Empty list subscribes to all events

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Set to `false` to pause delivery without deleting the endpoint

email.title: Email
email.description: Email used when requesting the code.

cursor.title: Cursor
cursor.description: Pass as `cursor` query param to fetch the next page

has_more.title: Has More
has_more.description: True if more results exist beyond this page

event_type.description: The event type that triggered this delivery

status.title: Status
status.description: Computed status: `delivered` if delivered_at is set, `failed` if attempts >= max_retries, otherwise `pending`.

max_retries.title: Max Retries
max_retries.description: Maximum delivery attempts before marking as failed

response_status.title: Response Status
response_status.description: HTTP status code from the last delivery attempt

last_error.title: Last Error
last_error.description: Error message from the last failed attempt

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Time at which the event was created

delivered_at.title: Delivered At
delivered_at.description: Time at which the event was successfully delivered

failed_at.title: Failed At
failed_at.description: Time at which the event permanently failed (all retries exhausted)

next_retry_at.title: Next Retry At
next_retry_at.description: Expected time for the next delivery retry

ready.title: Ready
ready.description: True when the webhook is active and receiving events.

secret.title: Secret
secret.description: HMAC-SHA256 signing secret. Use to verify webhook payload signatures. See docs for verification examples.

events.title: Events
events.description: Subscribed event types. Empty means all events

disabled_reason.title: Disabled Reason
disabled_reason.description: Reason the webhook was disabled. `consecutive_failures` when auto-disabled after repeated delivery failures.

readiness.description: Readiness status with actionable steps if the webhook needs attention.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Time at which the endpoint was created

success.title: Success
success.description: `true` if the endpoint returned a 2xx status code

status_code.title: Status Code
status_code.description: HTTP status code returned by the endpoint

response_body.title: Response Body
response_body.description: Response body from the endpoint, truncated to 2000 characters

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message if delivery failed entirely

HTTPBearer.description: API key (`lb_` prefix) or access token (`at_` prefix). Pass as: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Account creation, authentication, profile, and readiness.

tags.name: Stripe
tags.description: Connect and manage Stripe payment configuration.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Create, list, and revoke API keys.

tags.name: Files
tags.description: Upload files for deliverables, covers, and avatars.

tags.name: Listings
tags.description: Create, list, get, update, and delete listings.

tags.name: Orders
tags.description: View buyer orders and payment details.

tags.name: Customers
tags.description: View and search buyers who have completed orders.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Configure webhook endpoints for event notifications.
